name: NeurIPS / ICML / ICLR
notes: NeurIPS single-column format. 9-page limit (main content, excluding refs/appendix). Requires neurips_2024.sty file. Double-blind review. Appendix allowed for supplementary proofs/experiments.
writing_guide:
TONE: Precise but accessible to the broad ML community. Active voice ('We show...', 'Our method...'). Mathematical rigor expected but balance formalism with intuition. After every theorem or formal definition, provide an intuitive explanation.

ABSTRACT: 200-250 words. Lead with the problem's importance, state your specific contribution concretely, give 1-2 headline numbers. Mention if you provide theoretical guarantees.

STRUCTURE: 9 pages demands ruthless concision. Move proofs to Appendix and state only theorem statements in main text. Include: (1) clear problem formulation with notation table, (2) method with theoretical motivation, (3) comprehensive experiments. 'Broader Impact' statement required. Reproducibility checklist must be filled.

CITATIONS: 30-50 references. Cite foundational ML papers and recent top-venue work. Use \citet and \citep. Related Work can go after Introduction or before Conclusion; choose based on narrative flow.

FIGURES/TABLES: 5-8 display items in main text; more in appendix. Include: training curves, ablation tables, comparison tables with mean +/- std. Visualizations of learned representations are valued. Use consistent color schemes.

REVIEWER EXPECTATIONS: Ablation studies isolating each contribution. Statistical significance (multiple seeds, error bars). Comparison against strong recent baselines (not just 2+ year old methods). Computational cost analysis. Theoretical claims need proofs (appendix OK).

DO NOT: Reveal identity (double-blind). Do not skip ablations. Do not report results from single runs without justification. Do not claim SOTA without rigorous comparison. Do not exceed 9 pages for main content.
